days.title: Spring Core - IoC & DI
learningObjectives: Explain Spring Core - IoC & DI with phase-5 depth and clarity
Implement Java examples for Spring Core - IoC & DI with production awareness
Identify failure risks and mitigation strategies
Reason about trade-offs under realistic constraints
Design or optimize solutions with maintainability in mind
Present interview answers with concrete evidence and structure

sections.title: Why Spring Core - IoC & DI matters
sections.content:
Spring Core - IoC & DI is a high-value Day 38 topic in Phase 5 (Spring ecosystem and enterprise backend patterns).

At this stage, interviewers evaluate whether you can connect implementation details with reliability, scalability, and maintainability outcomes.

What strong answers include:
1. precise concept framing,
2. operational implications,
3. one realistic failure pattern,
4. one practical mitigation.

This topic is not just about code that compiles. It is about decisions that survive production traffic and team ownership.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Spring Core - IoC & DI
sections.content:
### Day 38: Spring Core - IoC & DI
Phase context: Spring ecosystem and enterprise backend patterns.

### What to understand deeply
- mechanism and boundaries
- runtime behavior and cost
- operational failure modes
- trade-offs vs alternatives

### Implementation workflow
1. Build a clear baseline solution.
2. Validate edge/failure paths.
3. Measure impact (latency, memory, throughput, or complexity).
4. Refactor for readability and maintainability.

### Interview framing template
- "The problem this solves is..."
- "I chose this approach because..."
- "The key trade-off is..."
- "A failure case is... and mitigation is..."

### Production perspective
For Spring Core - IoC & DI, quality is judged by correctness + resilience + debuggability, not just API usage.

sections.title: Basic Walkthrough
sections.description: Manual constructor injection (Spring-style idea without framework): depend on interface.

sections.title: Intermediate Implementation
sections.description: Simple service locator map: string key -> bean (mini IoC container).

sections.title: Advanced Production Pattern
sections.description: Decorator pattern: wrap a bean to add cross-cutting behavior (AOP-like).

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Relying on Spring defaults without understanding lifecycle and bean scope.
Leaking transaction boundaries across service methods.
Ignoring lazy loading and query shape in persistence logic.
Implementing security rules without end-to-end flow clarity.
Using framework annotations mechanically without design intent.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Core - IoC & DI Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Core - IoC & DI**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Inversion of Control?
conceptual.answer: Framework controls flow and calls your code—contrasts with library you call.

conceptual.question: What is Dependency Injection?
conceptual.answer: Dependencies provided from outside (constructor/setter) rather than created internally—testability.

conceptual.question: What is Spring ApplicationContext?
conceptual.answer: IoC container managing bean lifecycle and wiring.

conceptual.question: What is @Autowired?
conceptual.answer: Injection by type (and qualifier)—constructor injection preferred for immutability.

conceptual.question: What is @Component @Service @Repository?
conceptual.answer: Stereotypes marking Spring beans—semantics and AOP proxies differ slightly.

conceptual.question: What is @Configuration @Bean?
conceptual.answer: Java-based bean definitions replacing XML.

conceptual.question: What is bean scope singleton default?
conceptual.answer: One instance per container—stateless services ideal.

conceptual.question: What is @Primary @Qualifier?
conceptual.answer: Disambiguate multiple beans of same type.

conceptual.question: What is circular dependency problem?
conceptual.answer: Bean A needs B needs A—constructor injection fails; refactor or setter injection cautiously.

conceptual.question: What is ApplicationContext vs BeanFactory?
conceptual.answer: ApplicationContext superset—events, i18n, eager init of singletons by default.

conceptual.question: What is stereotype component scanning?
conceptual.answer: Classpath scanning for annotated classes—@SpringBootApplication includes.

conceptual.question: What is PropertySources and @Value?
conceptual.answer: Externalize configuration—12-factor app pattern.

conceptual.question: What is Spring profiles?
conceptual.answer: Beans activated per environment—dev, test, prod.

conceptual.question: What is conditional beans @Conditional?
conceptual.answer: Register beans based on classpath, properties, etc.

conceptual.question: What is FactoryBean?
conceptual.answer: Advanced indirection creating complex beans—rare interview depth.

codeBased.question: ApplicationContext getBean usage—when?
codeBased.answer: Generally avoid in application code—prefer injection.

seniorScenario.question: Huge Spring context slow startup?
seniorScenario.answer: Lazy beans, trimming classpath, spring-native/AOT strategies.

seniorScenario.question: Wrong bean in prod—debug?
seniorScenario.answer: Actuator beans endpoint, conditional report, logging.

seniorScenario.question: Library brings unwanted component scan?
seniorScenario.answer: Exclude auto-configurations carefully—explicit imports.

seniorScenario.question: Testing without Spring context—approach?
seniorScenario.answer: Pure unit tests with manual mocks vs @SpringBootTest slices.

seniorScenario.question: Microservices shared library Spring configs conflict?
seniorScenario.answer: Namespace properties, starter modules discipline.

seniorScenario.question: Memory leak Spring singleton holding HTTP session?
seniorScenario.answer: Design stateless services; scope session carefully.

wrongAnswers: @Autowired always injects by name first, never by type.
Spring beans are always prototype scope unless specified.
You should call new on @Service classes in your code.
IoC means objects create themselves more easily.
ApplicationContext and BeanFactory are identical in features.
Constructor injection cannot be used with final fields.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 5 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Spring bean lifecycle?
conceptual.answer: Instantiate → populate properties → initialize callbacks → use → destroy.

conceptual.question: What is BeanPostProcessor?
conceptual.answer: Hook to wrap/modify beans during creation—advanced extension.

conceptual.question: What is InitializingBean vs @PostConstruct?
conceptual.answer: Callbacks after properties set—prefer standard annotations.

conceptual.question: What is DisposableBean / @PreDestroy?
conceptual.answer: Cleanup on shutdown—close connections.

conceptual.question: What is Aware interfaces?
conceptual.answer: Bean gets ApplicationContext, Environment—use sparingly.

conceptual.question: What is lazy initialization?
conceptual.answer: Bean created on first request—faster startup vs first-hit latency.

conceptual.question: What is @DependsOn?
conceptual.answer: Force bean creation order when implicit wiring insufficient.

conceptual.question: What is FactoryBean vs factory method?
conceptual.answer: Spring FactoryBean special interface vs @Bean method factory pattern.

conceptual.question: What is bean definition inheritance?
conceptual.answer: parent attribute in XML—less common in annotation era.

conceptual.question: What is @Scope proxyMode?
conceptual.answer: Scoped proxies for singleton injecting request scope bean—interface required.

conceptual.question: What is ObjectProvider<T>?
conceptual.answer: Lazy lookup of bean or multiple candidates—avoid eager resolution.

conceptual.question: What is Provider<T> injection?
conceptual.answer: JSR-330 lazy retrieval—similar use cases.

conceptual.question: What is @RefreshScope (Cloud)?
conceptual.answer: Recreate bean on config refresh—state careful.

conceptual.question: What is AOT processing Spring 6?
conceptual.answer: Ahead-of-time hints for native image—startup implications.

conceptual.question: What is bean overriding default behavior?
conceptual.answer: Spring Boot 2.1+ false by default—prevent accidental replace.

codeBased.question: BeanPostProcessor wrap with proxy example concept.
@Configuration proxyBeanMethods false why?
codeBased.answer: Avoid CGLIB overhead when no cross-bean calls.

seniorScenario.question: Startup failure missing bean after refactor?
seniorScenario.answer: Conditional missing property; use --debug or failure analyzer.

seniorScenario.question: BeanPostProcessor order issues?
seniorScenario.answer: Implement Ordered or @Order—ordering bugs subtle.

seniorScenario.question: Hot reload devtools restart vs live reload?
seniorScenario.answer: Classloader restart vs resource refresh—limitations.

seniorScenario.question: Cloud native buildpacks lifecycle vs Spring lifecycle?
seniorScenario.answer: Container build layers separate concerns.

seniorScenario.question: Memory leak static field holding BeanFactory?
seniorScenario.answer: Avoid static references to context.

seniorScenario.question: GraalVM native image reflection on beans?
seniorScenario.answer: Register hints for dynamic features—Spring Boot helps.

wrongAnswers: @PostConstruct runs before dependency injection.
Singleton beans are destroyed after every HTTP request.
BeanPostProcessor is used in every typical business service.
Lazy beans are never proxies.
DisposableBean is the only way to run code on shutdown.
@Configuration classes are never subclassed by Spring CGLIB.

Implement a realistic solution for **Spring AOP**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.

3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is AOP?
conceptual.answer: Aspect-Oriented Programming—cross-cutting concerns like logging, transactions, security modularized.

conceptual.question: What is advice, pointcut, aspect?
conceptual.answer: Advice is action; pointcut matches join points; aspect bundles them.

conceptual.question: What is @Around advice?
conceptual.answer: Wraps method—can proceed or short-circuit—most powerful.

conceptual.question: What is proxy-based AOP in Spring?
conceptual.answer: JDK dynamic proxies (interfaces) or CGLIB (classes)—method calls intercepted.

conceptual.question: What limitations of Spring AOP?
conceptual.answer: Only public method calls through proxy intercepted—self-invocation bypasses aspect.

conceptual.question: What is @Transactional AOP?
conceptual.answer: Declarative transaction boundaries—rollback rules, propagation, isolation.

conceptual.question: What is pointcut expression language?
conceptual.answer: execution(...), @annotation, within, args—AspectJ syntax subset.

conceptual.question: What is weaving?
conceptual.answer: Compile-time, load-time, or runtime proxy—weaving differs; Spring uses proxy at runtime.

conceptual.question: What is Advice order @Order?
conceptual.answer: Multiple aspects on same join point—ordering matters.

conceptual.question: What is introduction in AOP?
conceptual.answer: Declare additional interface implementation on existing bean—advanced.

conceptual.question: What is TargetSource?
conceptual.answer: Advanced proxy target swapping—rare.

conceptual.question: What is self-invocation problem fix?
conceptual.answer: Inject self proxy @Lazy or split service; or AspectJ compile-time weaving.

conceptual.question: Performance overhead AOP?
conceptual.answer: Usually small—measure hot paths; avoid heavy work in aspects.

conceptual.question: What is @EnableAspectJAutoProxy?
conceptual.answer: Enables aspect processing in Spring Boot auto-config included.

conceptual.question: What is transaction proxy with rollbackFor?
conceptual.answer: Checked exceptions don’t rollback by default—configure.

codeBased.question: Test aspect applied—use @SpringBootTest assert advice called.
codeBased.answer: Or unit test aspect in isolation.

seniorScenario.question: Transaction not rolling back—cause?
seniorScenario.answer: Caught exception, self-invocation, wrong exception type, read-only resource.

seniorScenario.question: Security audit aspect silently failing open?
seniorScenario.answer: Dangerous—fail closed; monitor aspect health.

seniorScenario.question: Latency added by metrics aspect?
seniorScenario.answer: Sampling, async export, avoid heavy serialization in advice.

seniorScenario.question: Monolithic God aspect doing everything?
seniorScenario.answer: Split concerns—single responsibility aspects.

seniorScenario.question: Distributed transactions vs @Transactional local?
seniorScenario.answer: Different—Saga/outbox patterns for microservices.

seniorScenario.question: Upgrade Spring AOP behavior change?
seniorScenario.answer: Read release notes—proxy differences JDK versions.

wrongAnswers: Spring AOP intercepts all method calls including private and self-invoked ones.
@Transactional works on private methods reliably.
Aspects are only for logging, not transactions.
JDK dynamic proxies work for any class without interfaces.
Around advice must always call proceed() exactly once.
Pointcuts are evaluated at compile time for Spring AOP.

Implement a realistic solution for **Spring MVC & REST**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Spring MVC?
conceptual.answer: Web framework mapping HTTP requests to @Controller/@RestController methods with views or bodies.

conceptual.question: What is DispatcherServlet?
conceptual.answer: Front controller routing to handlers, resolvers, exception handlers.

conceptual.question: What is @RequestMapping variants?
conceptual.answer: @GetMapping @PostMapping shorthand for HTTP methods and paths.

conceptual.question: What is @RequestBody @ResponseBody?
conceptual.answer: JSON serialization with HttpMessageConverters—REST APIs.

conceptual.question: What is content negotiation?
conceptual.answer: Choosing representation via Accept header, extensions, or param—JSON/XML.

conceptual.question: What is HandlerMethodArgumentResolver?
conceptual.answer: Resolves @RequestParam, @PathVariable, @RequestHeader into method args.

conceptual.question: What is exception handling @ControllerAdvice?
conceptual.answer: Global @ExceptionHandler for consistent error responses.

conceptual.question: What is validation @Valid?
conceptual.answer: Bean Validation triggered on request bodies—400 on failure.

conceptual.question: What is servlet filter vs interceptor?
conceptual.answer: Filters servlet spec all requests; Spring MVC interceptors finer after dispatch.

conceptual.question: What is REST idempotency for GET/PUT/DELETE?
conceptual.answer: GET safe; PUT/DELETE idempotent—POST not guaranteed.

conceptual.question: What is status code choice?
conceptual.answer: 201 Created with Location, 204 No Content, 400 vs 422 semantics team choice.

conceptual.question: What is HATEOAS?
conceptual.answer: Hypermedia links in responses—maturity level 3 Richardson.

conceptual.question: What is multipart file upload?
conceptual.answer: MultipartResolver handles file parts—size limits security.

conceptual.question: What is async MVC Callable/DeferredResult?
conceptual.answer: Release servlet thread during long work—scalability.

conceptual.question: What is CORS in Spring?
conceptual.answer: CrossOrigin annotation or global WebMvcConfigurer—preflight OPTIONS.

seniorScenario.question: Giant controller class—refactor?
seniorScenario.answer: Split by domain, use @ControllerAdvice, service layer fat not controller.

seniorScenario.question: OpenAPI contract vs implementation drift?
seniorScenario.answer: Codegen or contract tests; CI validation.

seniorScenario.question: SSE streaming endpoint memory?
seniorScenario.answer: Backpressure, disconnect handling, heartbeats.

seniorScenario.question: Global exception hides root cause?
seniorScenario.answer: Log with correlation id; sanitize client message.

seniorScenario.question: API versioning URI vs header?
seniorScenario.answer: Trade-offs caching vs cleanliness—document policy.

seniorScenario.question: CSRF disabled for REST—safe?
seniorScenario.answer: Only stateless token APIs with proper auth—not for cookie session forms.

wrongAnswers: @Controller always serializes return values to JSON without annotations.
DispatcherServlet is not part of Spring MVC.
PUT requests must never create resources.
@RequestBody works for GET requests by default.
Spring MVC replaces the Servlet API entirely.
ResponseEntity can only return 200 OK.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Relying on Spring defaults without understanding lifecycle and bean scope.
Leaking transaction boundaries across service methods.
Ignoring lazy loading and query shape in persistence logic.
Implementing security rules without end-to-end flow clarity.
Using framework annotations mechanically without design intent.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Boot Internals Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Boot Internals**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is Spring Boot auto-configuration?
conceptual.answer: Classpath conditions register beans automatically—convention over configuration.

conceptual.question: What is spring.factories / AutoConfiguration.imports?
conceptual.answer: Mechanism to load auto-config classes—Boot 3 uses imports file.

conceptual.question: What is @SpringBootApplication?
conceptual.answer: Combines @Configuration @EnableAutoConfiguration @ComponentScan.

conceptual.question: What is embedded servlet container?
conceptual.answer: Tomcat/Jetty/Undertow embedded—fat JAR runnable.

conceptual.question: What is SpringApplication.run?
conceptual.answer: Bootstrap context, environment, logging, banner.

conceptual.question: What is Actuator endpoints?
conceptual.answer: Health, metrics, info, env—production observability.

conceptual.question: What is management.server.port separate?
conceptual.answer: Expose actuator on different port for security.

conceptual.question: What is graceful shutdown?
conceptual.answer: Complete in-flight requests before exit—server.* lifecycle.

conceptual.question: What is Spring Boot DevTools?
conceptual.answer: Restart on classpath change—exclude from prod.

conceptual.question: What is spring-boot-starter-parent?
conceptual.answer: Dependency management BOM versions.

conceptual.question: What is conditional annotations?
conceptual.answer: @ConditionalOnClass, OnMissingBean—auto-config decisions.

conceptual.question: What is lazy initialization spring.main.lazy-initialization?
conceptual.answer: Beans lazy until first use—startup vs first request latency.

conceptual.question: What is Spring Boot testing @SpringBootTest?
conceptual.answer: Full application context—heavy vs slices.

conceptual.question: What is build plugins spring-boot-maven-plugin?
conceptual.answer: Repackage fat jar, layered jars for Docker.

conceptual.question: What is native image support?
conceptual.answer: GraalVM AOT—smaller footprint faster startup constraints.

seniorScenario.question: Actuator endpoints exposed publicly—incident?
seniorScenario.answer: Lock down, auth, network policies, disable sensitive env dump.

seniorScenario.question: Fat JAR vs thin JAR vs native trade-offs?
seniorScenario.answer: Startup, memory, build time—choose per deployment.

seniorScenario.question: Auto-config bean conflict after upgrade?
seniorScenario.answer: Release notes, exclude, define own bean @Primary.

seniorScenario.question: Kubernetes liveness uses actuator health—pitfall?
seniorScenario.answer: Don’t mark down dependencies you want to restart for—separate readiness.

seniorScenario.question: Secrets in application.yml committed?
seniorScenario.answer: Use externalized config vault, env vars, sealed secrets.

seniorScenario.question: Slow Spring Boot startup 5 min—where look?
seniorScenario.answer: Bean creation logs, classpath scanning breadth, database on startup.

wrongAnswers: Spring Boot eliminates the need for a servlet container at runtime.
Auto-configuration cannot be disabled or overridden.
Actuator should always expose all endpoints on the main port without auth.
@SpringBootApplication scans every package on the classpath automatically.
spring-boot-starter includes only compile-time dependencies.
DevTools should be enabled in production for faster restarts.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 5 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is externalized configuration priority?
conceptual.answer: Command line, env vars, application-{profile}, defaults—later can override earlier depending on mechanism.

conceptual.question: What is @ConfigurationProperties vs @Value?
conceptual.answer: Bulk typed binding vs individual strings—validation support.

conceptual.question: What is relaxed binding?
conceptual.answer: kebab-case, camelCase, env var mapping—SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL style.

conceptual.question: What is Actuator /env /configprops?
conceptual.answer: Inspect resolved properties—debug mismatches.

conceptual.question: What is refresh scope (Cloud)?
conceptual.answer: Refresh beans on /refresh—@RefreshScope beans recreated.

conceptual.question: What is config server (Spring Cloud)?
conceptual.answer: Centralized git-backed config for distributed systems.

conceptual.question: What is encryption of properties?
conceptual.answer: Cipher values with key management—bootstrap carefully.

conceptual.question: What is Kubernetes ConfigMap vs Secret?
conceptual.answer: Mount as env or volume—Spring Cloud Kubernetes integration.

conceptual.question: What is spring.config.import?
conceptual.answer: Import additional property files or config tree—Boot 2.4+.

conceptual.question: What is random.* placeholder?
conceptual.answer: Random values for secrets in dev—don’t use for real secrets uncontrolled.

conceptual.question: What is logging.file.name?
conceptual.answer: File output rotation with logback/spring defaults.

conceptual.question: What is management.metrics export?
conceptual.answer: Micrometer to Prometheus, etc.—enable registry.

conceptual.question: What is info.* build properties?
conceptual.answer: Expose version via /info from build plugin metadata.

conceptual.question: What is spring.docker.compose?
conceptual.answer: Boot 3.1+ optional compose integration for dev—testcontainers overlap.

conceptual.question: What is feature flags integration?
conceptual.answer: Toggle beans via properties—LaunchDarkly etc.

seniorScenario.question: Wrong database URL in prod—how slipped?
seniorScenario.answer: Missing env in deployment manifest; add validation on startup fail-fast.

seniorScenario.question: Secret in git history—response?
seniorScenario.answer: Rotate credentials, purge history, secret scanning CI.

seniorScenario.question: Config drift across regions?
seniorScenario.answer: GitOps single source; diff environments with tooling.

seniorScenario.question: Actuator env exposure GDPR?
seniorScenario.answer: PII in properties—restrict endpoints, audit access.

seniorScenario.question: Hot reload config without restart—limits?
seniorScenario.answer: Only @RefreshScope beans; many components not refreshable.

seniorScenario.question: Micrometer cardinality explosion—symptom?
seniorScenario.answer: High memory in Prometheus—label discipline.

wrongAnswers: application.properties always overrides environment variables.
@Value cannot use default values.
Actuator /env should be public for easier debugging in production.
ConfigurationProperties works on any private field without setters.
spring.config.location only accepts classpath: resources.
Random values in application.yml are cryptographically secure secrets.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Relying on Spring defaults without understanding lifecycle and bean scope.
Leaking transaction boundaries across service methods.
Ignoring lazy loading and query shape in persistence logic.
Implementing security rules without end-to-end flow clarity.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Data JPA & Hibernate Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Data JPA & Hibernate**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is JPA?
conceptual.answer: Java Persistence API—ORM mapping entities to relational tables.

conceptual.question: What is Hibernate?
conceptual.answer: Popular JPA implementation—Session/EntityManager wraps JDBC.

conceptual.question: What is EntityManager?
conceptual.answer: Persistence context managing entity lifecycle—persist, merge, remove, find.

conceptual.question: What is @Entity @Table @Id?
conceptual.answer: Map class to table; primary key definition.

conceptual.question: What is lazy vs eager fetching?
conceptual.answer: Lazy loads associations on access—N+1 risk; eager loads immediately—overfetch risk.

conceptual.question: What is N+1 query problem?
conceptual.answer: One query for parent list + N queries for each child—fix with join fetch or batch size.

conceptual.question: What is JPQL vs Criteria API?
conceptual.answer: String queries vs programmatic query building—type-safe criteria.

conceptual.question: What is first-level cache?
conceptual.answer: Persistence context per transaction—identity map.

conceptual.question: What is second-level cache?
conceptual.answer: SessionFactory-level cache across transactions—EhCache, Infinispan—explicit config.

conceptual.question: What is @Transactional propagation isolation?
conceptual.answer: REQUIRED default; isolation READ_COMMITTED typical.

conceptual.question: What is optimistic locking @Version?
conceptual.answer: Detect concurrent updates with version column—OptimisticLockException.

conceptual.question: What is cascade types?
conceptual.answer: PERSIST, MERGE, REMOVE—dangerous if misused—orphanRemoval.

conceptual.question: What is fetch join in JPQL?
conceptual.answer: JOIN FETCH to load association in same query—avoid N+1.

conceptual.question: What is @Embeddable?
conceptual.answer: Value types composed into entity—shared table columns.

conceptual.question: What is inheritance mapping strategies?
conceptual.answer: SINGLE_TABLE, JOINED, TABLE_PER_CLASS—trade-offs normalization vs simplicity.

codeBased.question: @Query("select u from User u join fetch u.roles")
Map native query to DTO SqlResultSetMapping or interface projection

seniorScenario.question: Production Hibernate OOM loading graph?
seniorScenario.answer: Eager accidental; fetch plans; pagination; projections.

seniorScenario.question: Long transaction holding DB connection?
seniorScenario.answer: Split transaction; async outbox; reduce scope.

seniorScenario.question: Schema migration Flyway vs Liquibase choice?
seniorScenario.answer: SQL-first vs XML—team preference; both fine.

seniorScenario.question: Lost updates without versioning?
seniorScenario.answer: Add @Version; retry on conflict UX.

seniorScenario.question: Leaking EntityManager across threads?
seniorScenario.answer: Not thread-safe—one per request typically in Spring.

seniorScenario.question: Database round trips microservice chatty ORM?
seniorScenario.answer: Batch operations, jdbcTemplate for hot paths, caching.

wrongAnswers: JPA entities are always detached from the persistence context.
Lazy loading works the same after the EntityManager is closed.
Hibernate and JPA are the same thing.
@Transactional on private methods works in Spring.
N+1 problem means you have N+1 databases.
Second-level cache is enabled by default for all entities.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Relying on Spring defaults without understanding lifecycle and bean scope.
Leaking transaction boundaries across service methods.
Ignoring lazy loading and query shape in persistence logic.
Implementing security rules without end-to-end flow clarity.
Using framework annotations mechanically without design intent.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Data Transactions & Locking Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Data Transactions & Locking**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is @Transactional boundary?
conceptual.answer: Starts commit/rollback around method—proxy-based in Spring.

conceptual.question: What is propagation REQUIRED?
conceptual.answer: Join existing transaction or create new—default.

conceptual.question: What is REQUIRES_NEW?
conceptual.answer: Suspend outer and create new—inner commits independently.

conceptual.question: What is readOnly=true?
conceptual.answer: Hint for optimization; no guarantee across all drivers.

conceptual.question: What is isolation level?
conceptual.answer: READ_UNCOMMITTED to SERIALIZABLE—phantom vs dirty reads trade-offs.

conceptual.question: What is rollbackFor checked exception?
conceptual.answer: Default rollback unchecked only—configure for IOException etc.

conceptual.question: What is local vs global (JTA) transaction?
conceptual.answer: Single resource vs distributed XA—avoid XA if possible—Saga/outbox.

conceptual.question: What is pessimistic lock?
conceptual.answer: SELECT FOR UPDATE—serializes access—deadlock risk.

conceptual.question: What is optimistic lock @Version?
conceptual.answer: Compare-and-set on commit—retry on conflict.

conceptual.question: What is transaction timeout?
conceptual.answer: Prevent long locks—configure per transaction manager.

conceptual.question: What is savepoint nested transaction?
conceptual.answer: JDBC savepoints—not full nested transactions in all engines.

conceptual.question: What is @Transactional self-invocation issue?
conceptual.answer: Internal call bypasses proxy—same as AOP—split bean.

conceptual.question: What is connection pool HikariCP?
conceptual.answer: Fast pool—size = connections not threads—tune max pool vs DB max connections.

conceptual.question: What is leak detection threshold?
conceptual.answer: Hikari warns when connection held too long—debug leaks.

conceptual.question: What is transaction synchronization afterCommit?
conceptual.answer: Register callback run after successful commit—outbox pattern.

codeBased.question: REQUIRES_NEW on audit log service
codeBased.answer: Separate transaction commits even if outer rolls back—careful design.

codeBased.question: ChainedTransactionManager legacy
codeBased.answer: Multiple datasources—complex; prefer single or orchestration.

codeBased.question: @Transactional on test @Rollback default
codeBased.answer: Tests roll back—override if commit needed.

seniorScenario.question: Intermittent deadlock in DB—analysis?
seniorScenario.answer: Lock ordering, index scans, transaction size, retry with backoff idempotent.

seniorScenario.question: XA transaction performance poor—alternative?
seniorScenario.answer: Saga choreography, outbox pattern, eventual consistency.

seniorScenario.question: Connection pool exhausted under load?
seniorScenario.answer: Tune pool, reduce transaction duration, check leaks, scale DB connections.

seniorScenario.question: Read replica lag wrong reads?
seniorScenario.answer: Sticky session or sync read-your-writes strategy; not pure transaction issue only.

seniorScenario.question: @Transactional on microservice REST call?
seniorScenario.answer: Doesn’t span network—distributed transaction myth.

seniorScenario.question: Long running batch @Transactional whole batch?
seniorScenario.answer: Bad—chunk commits, restartability.

wrongAnswers: @Transactional always rolls back on any Exception including checked by default.
REQUIRES_NEW joins the current transaction always.
Connection pool size should equal number of HTTP threads always.
Optimistic locking requires SELECT FOR UPDATE.
Nested transactions are fully supported in all databases the same way.
readOnly=true guarantees the database will reject writes.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Relying on Spring defaults without understanding lifecycle and bean scope.
Leaking transaction boundaries across service methods.
Ignoring lazy loading and query shape in persistence logic.
Implementing security rules without end-to-end flow clarity.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Security - Authentication Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Security - Authentication**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is authentication vs authorization?
conceptual.answer: Who you are vs what you may do—Spring Security separates concerns.

conceptual.question: What is SecurityFilterChain?
conceptual.answer: Ordered filters processing request—replaces XML security config.

conceptual.question: What is UserDetailsService?
conceptual.answer: Load user by username for authentication.

conceptual.question: What is PasswordEncoder?
conceptual.answer: BCrypt etc.—never store plaintext passwords.

conceptual.question: What is form login vs httpBasic vs OAuth2?
conceptual.answer: Different authentication mechanisms—choose per app type.

conceptual.question: What is CSRF protection?
conceptual.answer: Synchronizer token for session cookie based web—often disabled for stateless JWT APIs with care.

conceptual.question: What is method security @PreAuthorize?
conceptual.answer: SpEL expressions on methods—enable method security.

conceptual.question: What is SecurityContextHolder?
conceptual.answer: Thread-local current authentication—clear in async carefully.

conceptual.question: What is remember-me?
conceptual.answer: Persistent token cookie—security trade-offs.

conceptual.question: What is session fixation mitigation?
conceptual.answer: Change session id on login—Spring default behaviors.

conceptual.question: What is cors integration security?
conceptual.answer: Allow origins deliberately—wildcard with credentials dangerous.

conceptual.question: What is rate limiting?
conceptual.answer: Not core Spring Security—add Bucket4j, gateway, or WAF.

conceptual.question: What is principal in SecurityContext?
conceptual.answer: Typically UserDetails or OAuth2 principal object.

conceptual.question: What is anonymous authentication?
conceptual.answer: Special anonymous user when not logged in—authorize accordingly.

conceptual.question: What is logout handler?
conceptual.answer: Invalidate session, clear cookies, CSRF token rotation.

codeBased.question: httpBasic() or formLogin deprecated style vs lambda DSL

seniorScenario.question: JWT in localStorage XSS—risk?
seniorScenario.answer: Prefer httpOnly cookies or strict CSP; sanitize XSS.

seniorScenario.question: Broken access control IDOR—fix?
seniorScenario.answer: Authorize resource ownership server-side every time.

seniorScenario.question: Session replication cluster security?
seniorScenario.answer: Sticky sessions or shared session store; secure cookie flags.

seniorScenario.question: Default Spring Boot security open endpoints?
seniorScenario.answer: Actuator, error pages—verify lockdown.

seniorScenario.question: Pen test finding weak bcrypt rounds?
seniorScenario.answer: Increase strength; migrate hashes on login.

seniorScenario.question: Async @Async SecurityContext lost?
seniorScenario.answer: DelegatingSecurityContextAsyncTaskExecutor propagate context.

wrongAnswers: Spring Security replaces the need for HTTPS.
CSRF protection is irrelevant for all REST APIs.
PasswordEncoder stores passwords in reversible encryption.
@PreAuthorize works without enabling method security.
SecurityContextHolder is automatically propagated to all child threads without configuration.
Anonymous users have a null Authentication object always.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Area | Phase 5 Expectation | Quick Rule | Gotcha |
|---|---|---|---|
| Framing | Explain intent + constraints | Start from problem | API-only answer |
| Design | State trade-off explicitly | Compare alternatives | One-solution bias |
| Runtime | Mention cost impact | Time + space/ops | No performance reasoning |
| Failure | Name one incident pattern | Mitigation path | Happy-path only thinking |
| Communication | Be concise + concrete | Use one example | Generic buzzwords |

sections.title: Why Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation matters
sections.content:
Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation is a high-value Day 47 topic in Phase 5 (Spring ecosystem and enterprise backend patterns).

At this stage, interviewers evaluate whether you can connect implementation details with reliability, scalability, and maintainability outcomes.

What strong answers include:
1. precise concept framing,
2. operational implications,
3. one realistic failure pattern,
4. one practical mitigation.

This topic is not just about code that compiles. It is about decisions that survive production traffic and team ownership.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation
sections.content:
### Day 47: Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation
Phase context: Spring ecosystem and enterprise backend patterns.

### What to understand deeply
- mechanism and boundaries
- runtime behavior and cost
- operational failure modes
- trade-offs vs alternatives

### Implementation workflow
1. Build a clear baseline solution.
2. Validate edge/failure paths.
3. Measure impact (latency, memory, throughput, or complexity).
4. Refactor for readability and maintainability.

### Interview framing template
- "The problem this solves is..."
- "I chose this approach because..."
- "The key trade-off is..."
- "A failure case is... and mitigation is..."

### Production perspective
For Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation, quality is judged by correctness + resilience + debuggability, not just API usage.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
items: Relying on Spring defaults without understanding lifecycle and bean scope.
Leaking transaction boundaries across service methods.
Ignoring lazy loading and query shape in persistence logic.
Implementing security rules without end-to-end flow clarity.
Using framework annotations mechanically without design intent.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring Security - OAuth2 & Authorisation**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is OAuth2 roles?
conceptual.answer: Resource owner, client, authorization server, resource server—clear separation.

conceptual.question: What is authorization code flow?
conceptual.answer: Browser redirects to auth server, exchanges code for tokens—most secure for SPAs with PKCE.

conceptual.question: What is JWT structure?
conceptual.answer: Header.payload.signature—verify signature with JWK—check exp, aud, iss.

conceptual.question: What is refresh token rotation?
conceptual.answer: Issue new refresh on use—detect reuse theft.

conceptual.question: What is scope in OAuth2?
conceptual.answer: Delegated permissions—fine-grained access control.

conceptual.question: What is opaque token vs JWT?
conceptual.answer: Reference token introspected at server vs self-contained JWT—trade-offs.

conceptual.question: What is Spring Authorization Server?
conceptual.answer: Framework to build authorization server—separate from Spring Security resource server.

conceptual.question: What is resource server configuration?
conceptual.answer: Validate JWT on APIs—issuer-uri, jwk-set-uri in Boot.

conceptual.question: What is PKCE?
conceptual.answer: Proof key for code exchange—protects public clients.

conceptual.question: What is client credentials flow?
conceptual.answer: Machine-to-machine—no user—client id secret.

conceptual.question: What is consent screen?
conceptual.answer: User approves scopes—UX and compliance.

conceptual.question: What is SSO vs OAuth2?
conceptual.answer: SSO outcome; OAuth2/OIDC protocols—related but distinct vocabulary.

conceptual.question: What is OIDC id_token?
conceptual.answer: Identity assertion distinct from access_token for APIs.

conceptual.question: What is audience claim aud?
conceptual.answer: Token intended recipient resource—validate to prevent misuse.

conceptual.question: What is confidential vs public client?
conceptual.answer: Can store secret vs cannot—PKCE required for public.

seniorScenario.question: Token stolen replay—mitigation?
seniorScenario.answer: Short TTL, rotation, binding, detect IP change, step-up auth.

seniorScenario.question: Misconfigured issuer-uri accepting wrong tokens?
seniorScenario.answer: Strict metadata validation; allowlist issuers.

seniorScenario.question: Microservices each validating JWT—key rotation?
seniorScenario.answer: Central JWK rotation with cache TTL; handle kid rollover.

seniorScenario.question: Compliance storing refresh tokens?
seniorScenario.answer: Encrypt at rest, device binding, user revocation UX.

seniorScenario.question: BFF pattern vs public SPA direct token?
seniorScenario.answer: Trade-offs XSS, deployment complexity.

seniorScenario.question: Breaking OAuth2 client change—semver?
seniorScenario.answer: Redirect URI changes coordinated with IdP.

wrongAnswers: JWTs cannot be revoked before expiry.
Client secret is safe to embed in single-page applications.
OAuth2 is only for user login buttons, not machine auth.
Resource server should trust any JWT signed by anyone if signature verifies.
Authorization code flow works without redirect URIs.
Refresh tokens should never be rotated.

sections.title: Exercise - Spring WebFlux & Reactive Applied Scenario
sections.problem:
Implement a realistic solution for **Spring WebFlux & Reactive**.

Requirements:
1. Define assumptions and constraints first.
2. Include one failure-path handling strategy.
3. Provide one improvement over baseline.
4. State complexity or runtime impact.
5. Keep implementation review-friendly.
hints: Add one observability/debug aid in output or comments.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is reactive programming?
conceptual.answer: Asynchronous data streams with backpressure—Publisher Subscriber.

conceptual.question: What is Project Reactor Mono vs Flux?
conceptual.answer: 0-1 vs 0-N elements—Spring WebFlux foundation.

conceptual.question: What is backpressure?
conceptual.answer: Slow consumer signals producer to slow—Reactive Streams Subscription.request.

conceptual.question: What is WebFlux vs MVC?
conceptual.answer: Non-blocking stack on Netty vs servlet threads—different programming model.

conceptual.question: What is RouterFunction?
conceptual.answer: Functional routing alternative to @RestController.

conceptual.question: What is block() danger?
conceptual.answer: Blocks event loop thread—defeats reactive benefits—use on bounded elastic scheduler.

conceptual.question: What is Schedulers.boundedElastic?
conceptual.answer: Offload blocking calls from reactive chain—limited threads.

conceptual.question: What is WebClient?
conceptual.answer: Non-blocking HTTP client—replace RestTemplate for reactive.

conceptual.question: What is R2DBC?
conceptual.answer: Reactive database driver—alternative to JDBC blocking.

conceptual.question: What is hot vs cold publisher?
conceptual.answer: Cold per subscriber; hot shared broadcast—different lifecycle.

conceptual.question: What is retry backoff in Reactor?
conceptual.answer: retryWhen with exponential backoff—transient errors.

conceptual.question: What is context propagation tracing?
conceptual.answer: Reactor Context for tracing ids across operators—MDC tricky.

conceptual.question: What is SSE in WebFlux?
conceptual.answer: Server-sent events streaming text/event-stream.

conceptual.question: What is functional endpoints WebFlux?
conceptual.answer: Cohesive routing handlers—testing style.

conceptual.question: When not use reactive?
conceptual.answer: Simple CRUD team unfamiliar—complexity cost; blocking stack fine at scale with threads.

codeBased.question: blockOptional last resort in test
codeBased.answer: Convert to sync in tests only.

seniorScenario.question: Accidentally called JDBC in WebFlux handler—symptom?
seniorScenario.answer: Thread starvation blocking event loop—move to boundedElastic or go MVC.

seniorScenario.question: Debugging reactive stack traces hard?
seniorScenario.answer: Checkpoint operator, structured logging, metrics.

seniorScenario.question: Mixing MVC and WebFlux in same app?
seniorScenario.answer: Possible in transition—careful thread model; not trivial.

seniorScenario.question: R2DBC connection pool sizing?
seniorScenario.answer: Different from JDBC—still tune; monitor acquisition time.

seniorScenario.question: Memory leaks cold vs hot Flux?
seniorScenario.answer: Hot must be disposed—subscription management.

seniorScenario.question: Grafana metrics reactive streams lag?
seniorScenario.answer: Backlog metrics; request concurrency limits.

wrongAnswers: WebFlux eliminates the need for thread pools entirely.
Mono can emit multiple elements like Flux.
block() is recommended in production WebFlux controllers.
Reactive Streams are only for browser WebSocket APIs.
RestTemplate is the recommended client for WebFlux services.
Backpressure means the server always pushes data faster.
